item.title: users unable to save new dives; some existing dives also affected
item.description:
Status: Resolved Affected: MotherDuck App
[Mar 05, 2026 23:06 UTC] Our internal monitors have detected an increase in server-side error messages immediately following a release. We are currently investigating the issue. So far a limited number of customers appear to be affected but the full impact is unknown.
[Mar 05, 2026 23:42 UTC] Following a code release at approximately 23:00 UTC, most customers are unable to create new dives and may experience errors when loading existing dives. We are continuing to investigate the root cause.
[Mar 05, 2026 23:59 UTC] We believe we have identified the root cause and are working to validate a fix. We believe the fix will be deployed within 2 hours.
[Mar 06, 2026 00:29 UTC] This incident has been resolved.

item.title: [Major] DuckLake Snapshot Deletions
item.description:
Status: Resolved Affected: MotherDuck App
[Feb 12, 2026 18:03 UTC] A bug was introduced during our recent production release at 7:24 UTC on Thursday, February 12 that has resulted in the premature deletion of ducklake snapshots in MotherDuck-managed ducklake S3 Buckets. We have identified the root cause and deployed a patch at approx 17:50 UTC.

item.title: [Minor] Intermittent Connection issues with Web UI
item.description:
Status: Resolved Affected: MotherDuck App
[Jan 08, 2026 16:39 UTC] We are investigating intermittent connection issues with the MotherDuck web UI.
Some notebook users are experiencing an error while loading attached databases. We're actively investigating. In the meantime, affected users can access their databases through other MotherDuck clients,...
[Jan 08, 2026 17:10 UTC] We have found the cause and are working on a mitigation.
[Jan 08, 2026 17:41 UTC] We have identified the problem and are working on deploying the fix for all customers.
[Jan 08, 2026 18:07 UTC] We are in the process of deploying a fix.
[Jan 08, 2026 18:36 UTC] We're in the process of deploying the fix.
[Jan 08, 2026 18:48 UTC] We've resolved the issue. The MotherDuck UI should be working as expected. If you have the UI in a browser tab, feel free to refresh the page.

item.title: [Minor] Applications with Wasm SDK versions 0.7 or older encountering errors
item.description:
Status: Resolved
[Jan 08, 2026 19:30 UTC] We've identified the root cause and are investigating a fix.
[Jan 08, 2026 19:58 UTC] We've resolved the issue. Older Wasm SDK versions should work as expected.
